CheckTitle: Linode account user has two-factor authentication enabled
Description: Linode account users are assessed for two-factor authentication (2FA) enablement. Users without 2FA are vulnerable to account takeover through credential theft or brute-force attacks.
Risk: Without two-factor authentication, a compromised password is sufficient for an attacker to gain full access to the user's Linode account, potentially leading to data exfiltration, resource destruction, or cryptocurrency mining abuse.

Code.Other:
1. Log in to the Linode Cloud Manager
2. Navigate to My Profile > Password & Authentication
3. Under Two-Factor Authentication, click Enable
4. Scan the QR code with an authenticator app
5. Enter the verification code to confirm

Recommendation.Text: Enable two-factor authentication for all Linode account users. Use an authenticator app (TOTP) for the second factor. Store backup codes securely.
